title: System Lockout — Retries Exceeded
shortDescription: The furnace locked out after multiple failed ignition attempts.
fullDescription: One LED blink on a Bryant furnace indicates ignition lockout. The system tried to light the burners but failed, entering a safety lockout. Bryant and Carrier share the same parent company, and many codes are similar.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: If you smell gas, leave immediately and call your gas company.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Cycle power off for 30 seconds, then on.

fixSteps.title: Clean Flame Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean with fine emery cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check Igniter
fixSteps.instruction: Observe glow during startup. Test resistance (10-200 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Verify Gas
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm gas valve is open and other appliances work.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and checking igniter does not resolve lockouts.

faq.q: Are Bryant and Carrier the same?
faq.a: Bryant and Carrier are sister brands made by the same parent company and share many parts and diagnostic codes.

faq.q: How do I reset the lockout?
faq.a: Cycle the furnace power off for 30 seconds, then back on.

faq.q: What is the most common fix?
faq.a: Cleaning the flame sensor is the most common fix for ignition lockout.

title: Pressure Switch Stuck Open
shortDescription: Pressure switch did not close — venting or draft problem.
fullDescription: Two blinks on a Bryant furnace indicate the pressure switch failed to close after the draft inducer motor started.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Never bypass the pressure switch.

fixSteps.title: Check Vents
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect outdoor vent pipes for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check Hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect pressure hose for cracks, water, disconnection.

fixSteps.title: Clear Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Flush condensate drain on 90%+ efficiency models.

fixSteps.title: Test Switch
fixSteps.instruction: Check continuity with inducer running.

whenToCallPro: If vents are clear and hose is OK but error persists.

title: Pressure Switch Stuck Closed
shortDescription: Pressure switch is closed before inducer starts.
fullDescription: Three blinks indicate the pressure switch is stuck closed before the inducer motor starts. This could be a stuck switch, water in the hose, or wiring fault.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: A stuck-closed switch defeats a safety check. Do not operate until fixed.

fixSteps.title: Drain Hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and drain the pressure switch hose.

fixSteps.title: Test Switch
fixSteps.instruction: With inducer off, should show no continuity.

fixSteps.title: Check Wires
fixSteps.instruction: Look for shorts across switch terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Replace if stuck closed.

whenToCallPro: If replacement does not fix it.

faq.a: Yes, trapped water holds the switch closed.
Match from the old switch or your Bryant model number.

title: Open High Limit Switch
fullDescription: Four blinks indicate the furnace overheated and the high limit switch opened to protect the heat exchanger. Usually an airflow problem.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Repeated overheating stresses the heat exchanger and risks CO leaks. Keep CO detectors working.

fixSteps.title: Replace Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the air filter.

fixSteps.title: Open Vents
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all vents are open and unblocked.

fixSteps.title: Check Blower
fixSteps.instruction: Set fan to ON — verify blower runs.

fixSteps.title: Clean Blower
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect and clean the blower wheel.

whenToCallPro: If filter is clean and vents are open but overheating continues.

faq.a: Yes, it can damage the heat exchanger over time.

title: Flame Without Gas Valve Command
shortDescription: Flame detected when gas valve should be closed.
fullDescription: Five blinks indicate flame is sensed when no gas should be flowing — a critical safety condition.
safetyWarning: CRITICAL: May indicate a gas leak. If you smell gas, leave and call 911.

fixSteps.title: Check Gas
fixSteps.instruction: If gas smell, leave and call 911.

fixSteps.title: Shut Down
fixSteps.instruction: Power off, gas off.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect flame sensor wire for grounding.

fixSteps.title: Call Pro
fixSteps.instruction: Professional diagnosis required.

title: Line Voltage Polarity Reversed
shortDescription: Reversed wiring polarity or missing ground detected.
fullDescription: Six blinks indicate incorrect polarity or grounding at the Bryant furnace power supply.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off breaker before checking wiring.

fixSteps.title: Power Off
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker.

fixSteps.title: Check Polarity
fixSteps.instruction: Verify hot/neutral connections.

fixSteps.title: Check Ground
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure ground is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test Supply
fixSteps.instruction: Test with outlet tester.

whenToCallPro: If not comfortable with electrical wiring.

faq.a: Use an outlet tester or multimeter.

title: Gas Valve Circuit Error
shortDescription: Fault in gas valve electrical circuit.
fullDescription: Seven blinks indicate a problem in the gas valve circuit — open coil, short, or wiring issue.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Do not attempt to manually operate the gas valve.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Off 60 seconds, then on.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all connections.

fixSteps.title: Test Coil
fixSteps.instruction: Measure valve coil resistance.

fixSteps.title: Call Pro
fixSteps.instruction: Gas valve replacement needs a licensed tech.

title: Igniter Circuit Open
fullDescription: Eight blinks indicate the hot surface igniter has an open circuit and needs replacement.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off furnace before handling igniter.

fixSteps.title: Inspect
fixSteps.instruction: Check for visible cracks.

fixSteps.title: Check Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure connector is firm.

fixSteps.title: Test
fixSteps.instruction: Good = 10-200 ohms; broken = OL.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Swap igniter. Handle by base only.

whenToCallPro: If new igniter does not fix it.

faq.a: In many cases yes, since Bryant and Carrier share parts. Verify the part number.

title: Grounding or Wiring Fault
shortDescription: Internal wiring or grounding fault detected.
fullDescription: Nine blinks indicate the control board detected an abnormal condition in the low or high voltage wiring.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off power and gas before inspecting wiring.

fixSteps.title: Power Off
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker and power switch.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check all wire connections for looseness or damage.

fixSteps.title: Check Ground
fixSteps.instruction: Verify ground wire is secure.

fixSteps.title: Look for Damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check for rodent damage or melted insulation.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot find the fault.

faq.q: How do I know if the board is bad?
faq.a: If all wiring is OK and the error persists, the board may be faulty.
Not typically, but do not operate the furnace until fixed.

title: No Power to Control Board
shortDescription: No LED — control board not receiving power.
fullDescription: No LED on a Bryant furnace means the board has no power. Check breaker, switch, fuse, transformer.
safetyWarning: Gas furnace safety: Turn off gas before electrical troubleshooting.

fixSteps.title: Check Breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Reset if tripped.

fixSteps.title: Check Switch
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure it is ON.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Replace if blown.

fixSteps.title: Test Transformer
fixSteps.instruction: Check 24VAC output.

faq.a: Yes, a short can blow the fuse.
